Title: ORCA3, a Jasmonate-Responsive Transcriptional Regulator of Plant Primary and Secondary Metabolism

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pyridoxal phosphate-dependent aromatic L-amino acid decarboxylase
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its expression is strongly induced by ORCA3 and it catalyzes the first committed step from tryptophan toward TIA biosynthesis, contributing to increased tryptamine production
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related and pharmaceutically valuable secondary metabolites
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA-inducible; elevated in Orca3-tagged and O3-OX lines
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increased in line 46 and O3-OX lines; induced by MeJA
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter assayed; CaMV 35S used for ORCA3, not TDC
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment; loganin feeding in downstream metabolite assay
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Northern blot; EMSA with promoter fragment; transient transactivation assay; metabolite profiling of tryptamine/TIAs
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate partner for ORCA3-based engineering to increase indole precursor flux into TIAs
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TDC is the first step enzyme from tryptophan into TIA biosynthesis. 2. Its expression was elevated in ORCA3 activation-tagged and overexpression lines. 3. ORCA3 directly bound and transactivated the Tdc promoter.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as selectable marker because elevated TDC confers 4-methyltryptophan resistance.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by ORCA3 and directly transactivated, promoting strictosidine formation for TIA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related and pharmaceutically valuable secondary metabolites
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA-inducible; elevated in Orca3-tagged and O3-OX lines
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increased in line 46 and O3-OX lines; induced by MeJA
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Northern blot; EMSA; transient transactivation assay; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for co-engineering TIA accumulation with ORCA3 and precursor-supply genes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STR expression increased in ORCA3 overexpression backgrounds. 2. ORCA3 bound and activated the Str promoter. 3. Enhanced STR likely contributed to strictosidine accumulation after loganin feeding.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the early key enzymes of TIA biosynthesis used to identify multi-gene regulatory activation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by ORCA3 and directly transactivated, supporting P450-dependent reactions in TIA metabolism
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related and pharmaceutically valuable secondary metabolites
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA-inducible; elevated in Orca3-tagged and O3-OX lines
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increased in line 46 and O3-OX lines; induced by MeJA
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Northern blot; EMSA; transient transactivation assay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential support gene to improve oxidative steps in engineered TIA pathways
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPR transcript was increased by ORCA3 overexpression. 2. ORCA3 specifically bound and transactivated the Cpr promoter. 3. CPR is part of the ORCA3-controlled TIA biosynthetic module.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports cytochrome P450 reactions rather than being a pathway-specific product-forming enzyme.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by ORCA3, indicating increased primary metabolic flux toward tryptophan precursor supply for TIA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related and pharmaceutically valuable secondary metabolites
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA-inducible; increased in ORCA3 overexpression background
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in line 46; increased in O3-OX lines
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Northern blot; expression quantification; associated metabolite profiling of tryptophan increase
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for increasing precursor supply when combined with downstream TIA pathway activation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ASα, a primary metabolism gene supplying TIA precursors, was induced by ORCA3. 2. Its induction supports coordinated regulation of primary and secondary metabolism. 3. Increased tryptophan accumulation is consistent with elevated upstream precursor synthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct promoter binding by ORCA3 was not shown in this paper.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by ORCA3, suggesting increased terpenoid precursor biosynthesis for TIA production
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related and pharmaceutically valuable secondary metabolites
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA-inducible; increased in ORCA3 overexpression background
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in line 46; increased in O3-OX lines
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment; loganin feeding reveals downstream impact on TIA accumulation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to combine with ORCA3, though additional bottlenecks such as G10H must be addressed
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DXS, a primary metabolism gene contributing to terpenoid precursor formation, was induced by ORCA3. 2. This supports ORCA3-mediated coordination of precursor supply with TIA biosynthesis. 3. Despite DXS induction, terpenoid precursor limitation remained, implying additional downstream bottlenecks.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Induction of DXS alone was insufficient to overcome the apparent G10H bottleneck.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression increased in ORCA3-tagged line, linking ORCA3 to downstream TIA conversion steps
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related and pharmaceutically valuable secondary metabolites
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elevated in line 46, but variable expression among controls
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible supporting gene in TIA pathway engineering
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SGD transcript increased in the Orca3-tagged line. 2. This suggests ORCA3 influences later TIA pathway steps beyond TDC and STR. 3. Interpretation is tempered by variable SGD expression in control lines.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note SGD showed variable expression in control cell lines.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by ORCA3, showing regulation extends to late vindoline-related TIA pathway genes
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related and pharmaceutically valuable secondary metabolites
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA-inducible; elevated in ORCA3 overexpression background
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increased in line 46 and O3-OX lines
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Indicates ORCA3 can be used to coordinate both early and late TIA pathway genes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D4H expression increased upon ORCA3 overexpression. 2. ORCA3 therefore affects not only early but also later vindoline-related pathway genes in suspension-cultured cells. 3. This broadens ORCA3 utility for pathway-level engineering.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper contrasts this with earlier reports of differential regulation of early versus late vindoline biosynthetic genes.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/EREBP family transcription factor; AP2 DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes terpenoid indole alkaloid accumulation and increases tryptophan/tryptamine levels by activating primary and secondary metabolic genes involved in TIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Jasmonate-responsive activator that coordinately induces multiple TIA-pathway and precursor-supply genes; sufficient to increase TIA accumulation when terpenoid precursor supply is provided
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Tdc, Str, Cpr directly; induces Sgd, D4h, ASα, and DXS; does not induce G10h, Dat, Ggpps, or Ics
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related secondary metabolites; many TIAs are pharmaceutically useful compounds
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of terpenoid indole alkaloids and precursor-supplying primary metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via sequence-specific promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Tdc promoter; Str promoter; Cpr promoter
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA-inducible in Catharanthus roseus suspension cells
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after 6 h treatment with 50 μM MeJA in MP183L cells; overexpressed in activation-tagged line 46 and CaMV 35S O3-OX lines
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) in overexpression and transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 50 μM MeJA treatment; extracellular loganin feeding (1 mM, 24 h) for TIA accumulation assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA activation tagging; constitutive overexpression in stable transgenic cell lines; Northern blot expression analysis; metabolite profiling; electrophoretic mobility shift assay; transient transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ORCA3 overexpression alone did not yield detectable TIAs without loganin feeding, indicating pathway bottleneck/precursor limitation at terpenoid branch
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory gene for metabolic engineering of TIA production, especially when stacked with genes improving terpenoid precursor supply such as the G10H branch or precursor feeding strategies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORCA3 was identified by T-DNA activation tagging as a jasmonate-responsive AP2-domain regulator in Catharanthus roseus. 2. ORCA3 overexpression increased expression of multiple TIA biosynthetic genes and precursor-pathway genes, including Tdc, Str, Cpr, Sgd, D4h, ASα, and DXS. 3. ORCA3 directly bound and transactivated Tdc, Str, and Cpr promoters. 4. ORCA3 increased tryptophan and tryptamine accumulation and enhanced TIA production after loganin feeding, demonstrating coordinated control of primary and secondary metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper distinguishes ORCA3-regulated genes from MeJA-induced but ORCA3-independent genes; G10h appears to be a bottleneck because it was not induced by ORCA3 and was undetectable in O3-OX lines.
